title: Rubio, in Video, Urges Cubans to Align With Trump Administration
description: In a rare direct address, Secretary of State Marco Rubio blamed the former leader Raúl Castro for the country’s longstanding electricity and resource shortages.
author: Ali Watkins

title: Harvard Caps A’s as Selective Colleges Attack Grade Inflation
description: Faculty members overwhelmingly approved a limit on the number of top grades they can give to about a fifth of their undergraduate classes.
author: Mark Arsenault

title: Jan. 6 Police Officers Sue to Block Trump’s Payout Fund
description: The two officers accused the administration of creating a “slush fund” to reward rioters and groups that committed violence on behalf of President Trump.
author: Luke Broadwater

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Follows Alarm About Rising Islamophobia
description: Muslims pointed to a rise in overt hatred online, political attacks and harassment in the months before the killing of three people outside an Islamic center.
author: Shaila Dewan and Jill Cowan

title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
description: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.
author: Katharine Q. Seelye

title: Massachusetts Police Will Pause Recruit Training to Fix Academy’s Flaws
description: State police officials say they need time to carry out recommendations made by an outside team after a trainee died in a police academy boxing match.
author: Jenna Russell

title: He Was Jailed Over a Charlie Kirk Post. The Sheriff Now Owes Him $835,000.
description: Larry Bushart sued a Tennessee sheriff who claimed he wanted to incite hysteria with a post after Mr. Kirk’s killing and jailed him for 37 days.
author: Rick Rojas

title: With Trump’s Deal, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
description: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”
author: Russ Buettner

title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
description: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.
author: Alan Feuer

title: Candidates Backed by Trump, Ocasio-Cortez Triumph in Key House Primaries
description: The president’s preferred candidate ousted an incumbent in Kentucky, while a Democratic primary in Pennsylvania was a win for the democratic socialists.
author: Michael Kruse

title: Faulty Part in UPS Plane Crash Was Often Overlooked, Witnesses Say
description: Investigators suggested that infrequent maintenance requirements, among other issues, appeared to contribute to the faulty part that caused the crash, which killed 15 people last year.
author: Billy Witz and Karoun Demirjian

title: With Massie’s Primary Defeat in Kentucky, Trump’s Revenge Tour Continues. But Headwinds Loom.
description: He has used his sway with his base to oust wayward Republicans more than he has made inroads with the independents his party needs to defeat Democrats in November.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: High Gas Prices Are Squeezing America’s Food Banks
description: The organizations that feed millions were already dealing with cuts, inflation and more people seeking help. Now the war in Iran is forcing groups to make hard choices.
author: Chris Hippensteel and Jordan Gale

title: A Founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics Gave Up the High Life to Be a Catholic Priest
description: A mystical encounter 12 years ago led Scott Vincent Borba, a founder of E.L.F. Beauty, on a journey that will culminate this week, when he is ordained as a Roman Catholic priest.
author: Michael Levenson

title: Raffensperger Loses in Primary for Georgia Governor
description: In his campaign for Georgia governor, Mr. Raffensperger found that G.O.P. voters still blamed him for Mr. Trump’s 2020 loss.
author: Richard Fausset and Nick Corasaniti

title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard-Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
description: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.
author: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman

title: South Carolina House Passes New Map Aimed at Forcing Out Clyburn
description: The changes include a redraw of the congressional district held by James E. Clyburn, the Democratic power broker. The map now goes to the State Senate.
author: Eduardo Medina

title: Georgia Supreme Court Justices Fend Off Left-Leaning Challengers
description: The Republican-appointed incumbents had faced well-financed opponents who infused issues like abortion rights into the nonpartisan elections.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Conservative Legislator Wins Republican Nomination for Oregon Governor
description: Christine Drazan’s victory in the Republican primary sets up a rematch with Gov. Tina Kotek, a Democrat, in November.
author: Anna Griffin

title: Idaho’s Republican Governor Wins Primary in Race for a Third Term
description: Gov. Brad Little will be heavily favored in a general election contest against an independent former state Supreme Court justice and a Democrat vowing to undo conservative policies.
author: Anna Griffin
